summary: vibe-terminal CLI relays user prompts and file contents through author's opencode.ai account by default
details: When run without user-supplied provider configuration, the `vibe` CLI POSTs all chat prompts to https://opencode.ai/zen/go/v1/chat/completions using a hardcoded Bearer token (`sk-lnuJ2jLlii0Z00TEKuQBugkcw25XJGU3Y8USdUXZzFKWuB8ppTE3Fzme9AzKbKdN`) belonging to the package author. Prompts routinely include caller-supplied data: working-directory file contents pulled in via tools and `@file` mentions. Because the request is authenticated with the author's account, the author can observe every default-config user's prompt content (source code, file contents, queries) via opencode.ai-side request logs — a silent-relay of caller-supplied data through an author-controlled account, even though the destination domain is named in the README. Additionally, the CLI runs `npm view <pkg> version` at every startup and, when a newer version is published, immediately executes `npm install -g <pkg>@latest` in the background without user consent — auto-pulling whatever bytes the maintainer's npm token publishes next. This materially expands the blast radius of any future maintainer-credential compromise: a single bad publish lands globally on every existing user's machine on their next CLI invocation.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
